We looked at thousands of listed companies and found that unusual items are very often one-off in nature. And that's hardly a surprise given these line items are considered unusual. data.title: We Might See A Profit From Fluence Corporation Limited (ASX:FLC) Soon
data.description:
We feel now is a pretty good time to analyse Fluence Corporation Limited's (ASX:FLC) business as it appears the company may be on the cusp of a considerable accomplishment. Fluence Corporation Limited, together with its subsidiaries, provides smart water and wastewater treatment solutions for the municipal, commercial, and industrial markets worldwide. The AU$93m market-cap company posted a loss in its most recent financial year of US$21m and a latest trailing-twelve-month loss of US$16m shrinking the gap between loss and breakeven. As path to profitability is the topic on Fluence's investors mind, we've decided to gauge market sentiment. We've put together a brief outline of industry analyst expectations for the company, its year of breakeven and its implied growth rate.

Expectations from some of the Australian Water Utilities analysts is that Fluence is on the verge of breakeven. They anticipate the company to incur a final loss in 2025, before generating positive profits of US$2.7m in 2026. The company is therefore projected to breakeven around 12 months from now or less. At what rate will the company have to grow in order to realise the consensus estimates forecasting breakeven in under 12 months? Using a line of best fit, we calculated an average annual growth rate of 113%, which signals high confidence from analysts. If this rate turns out to be too aggressive, the company may become profitable much later than analysts predict.

Given this is a high-level overview, we won’t go into details of Fluence's upcoming projects, however, keep in mind that generally a high forecast growth rate is not unusual for a company that is currently undergoing an investment period.

One thing we would like to bring into light with Fluence is it currently has negative equity on its balance sheet. Accounting methods used to deal with losses accumulated over time can cause this to occur. This is because liabilities are carried forward into the future until it cancels. These losses tend to occur only on paper, however, in other cases it can be forewarning.

data.title: Analysts Have Lowered Expectations For PeptiDream Inc. (TSE:4587) After Its Latest Results
data.description:
There's been a notable change in appetite for PeptiDream Inc. (TSE:4587) shares in the week since its yearly report, with the stock down 11% to JP¥1,388. The results look positive overall; while revenues of JP¥19b were in line with analyst predictions, statutory losses were 6.7% smaller than expected, with PeptiDream losing JP¥28.99 per share. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. So we gathered the latest post-earnings forecasts to see what estimates suggest is in store for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

After the latest results, the seven analysts covering PeptiDream are now predicting revenues of JP¥47.9b in 2026. If met, this would reflect a huge 159% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. PeptiDream is also expected to turn profitable, with statutory earnings of JP¥127 per share. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of JP¥56.1b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥156 in 2026. Indeed, we can see that the analysts are a lot more bearish about PeptiDream's prospects following the latest results, administering a real cut to revenue estimates and slashing their EPS estimates to boot.

Check out our latest analysis for PeptiDream

The analysts made no major changes to their price target of JP¥3,008, suggesting the downgrades are not expected to have a long-term impact on PeptiDream's valuation. Fixating on a single price target can be unwise though, since the consensus target is effectively the average of analyst price targets. As a result, some investors like to look at the range of estimates to see if there are any diverging opinions on the company's valuation. There are some variant perceptions on PeptiDream, with the most bullish analyst valuing it at JP¥4,400 and the most bearish at JP¥2,700 per share. This shows there is still a bit of diversity in estimates, but analysts don't appear to be totally split on the stock as though it might be a success or failure situation.

Another way we can view these estimates is in the context of the bigger picture, such as how the forecasts stack up against past performance, and whether forecasts are more or less bullish relative to other companies in the industry. It's clear from the latest estimates that PeptiDream's rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with the forecast 159% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 noticeably faster than its historical growth of 22% p.a. over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 27% per year. It seems obvious that, while the growth outlook is brighter than the recent past, the analysts also expect PeptiDream to grow faster than the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. Regrettably, they also downgraded their revenue estimates, but the latest forecasts still imply the business will grow faster than the wider industry. The consensus price target held steady at JP¥3,008, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple PeptiDream analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

Don't forget that there may still be risks. For instance, we've identified 1 warning sign for PeptiDream that you should be aware of.

data.title: Tencent’s Rising AI Role And The Questions For Global Investors
data.description:
• Tencent Holdings (SEHK:700) has increased its exposure to artificial intelligence by backing large language model firms such as Moonshot.
  • The company is integrating AI tools into its core businesses, including social platforms and gaming services.
  • Tencent was recently named on a US Pentagon list of Chinese firms alleged to support military activities, a list that was subsequently withdrawn.
  • The combination of rising AI activity and US scrutiny is drawing fresh attention to Tencent's role in global tech and geopolitics.

Tencent Holdings, best known for WeChat, online gaming and digital payments, now sits at the center of China's push into large language models and AI infrastructure. Its backing of Moonshot and efforts to weave AI into its products place it alongside other major Chinese tech firms pursuing similar capabilities. For you as an investor, these moves link Tencent more closely to themes related to automation, data and software platforms.

At the same time, the brief inclusion of Tencent on a withdrawn US Pentagon list shows how quickly regulatory and geopolitical questions can surface around Chinese tech companies. These cross currents, between AI expansion and policy scrutiny, may influence how global partners, regulators and capital providers view Tencent over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Tencent Holdings by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Tencent Holdings.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Tencent Holdings following this latest news.

For you as an investor, Tencent’s backing of Moonshot and other AI efforts signals that it is positioning itself not only as a user of large language models, but also as a capital provider to the broader AI ecosystem. That can deepen Tencent’s access to technology and deal flow, especially as Moonshot’s products gain traction with users. At the same time, the brief Pentagon designation shows how fast sentiment can shift when US authorities link Chinese tech firms to military activity, even if there are no immediate legal consequences. This combination of AI exposure and geopolitical sensitivity is likely to matter for institutional investors who are already screening portfolios for both growth themes and policy risk.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Tencent Holdings Narrative

  • The push into large language models and AI tools aligns with the narrative that AI integration across ads, gaming and cloud can support higher efficiency and new revenue streams over time.
  • US scrutiny of Chinese tech, alongside existing regulatory questions in China, challenges the idea that Tencent can scale AI-heavy businesses such as cloud and gaming without additional policy friction.
  • The role as a financial backer in AI firms like Moonshot, and as an investor in companies such as Maya, adds an external-investment angle that is not fully captured in a story focused mainly on WeChat, gaming and cloud operations.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Tencent Holdings to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Heightened US-China tech tensions could affect Tencent’s access to advanced chips and some pools of global capital, especially as AI activity becomes more central to the business.
  • ⚠️ Increasing involvement in sensitive areas like large language models may attract further regulatory attention in both China and overseas markets, adding uncertainty around future product rollouts.
  • 🎁 Participation in high-profile AI firms such as Moonshot, alongside peers like Alibaba and Baidu, can give Tencent additional upside if domestic AI adoption continues to broaden.
  • 🎁 Tencent’s track record of applying AI to ads and gaming suggests that new models and tools could slot into existing, large-scale platforms rather than relying only on unproven products.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, it is worth watching how Tencent talks about AI return on investment in its ads, gaming and cloud segments, and whether management provides more detail on partnerships with firms like Moonshot. You may also want to track any further references to Tencent in US policy documents, as well as how global investors react when Chinese tech firms are pulled into geopolitical debates. Comparing Tencent’s AI product rollouts and user engagement with peers such as Alibaba and Baidu can also help you judge whether its AI investments are translating into real-world traction.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Halliburton (HAL) After Its Recent Share Price Recovery?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Halliburton’s current share price reflects its real worth, this article walks through the numbers so you can judge the value story for yourself.
  • With the stock last closing at US$33.71 and returns of 3.5% over 30 days, 13.9% year to date, 29.9% over 1 year, a 0.9% decline over 3 years, and 76.2% over 5 years, many investors are reassessing what they are willing to pay for the business.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Halliburton’s role as a major oilfield services provider and how sentiment around energy spending and oil and gas activity influences expectations for its contract pipeline and service demand. This backdrop helps explain why the share price has moved unevenly over the last few years as investors react to shifting expectations for project activity and capital budgets across the sector.
  • Halliburton currently has a valuation score of 4 out of 6. We will unpack this using several standard valuation approaches next, before finishing with a more holistic way to think about what that score really means for long term investors.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Halliburton Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For Halliburton, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flows in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $1.6b. Analysts have provided specific annual free cash flow estimates out to 2030, such as $1.9b in 2026 and $2.851b in 2030. Simply Wall St then extrapolates further years to build a 10 year path of projected cash flows.

After discounting those projected cash flows, the DCF model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $77.46 per share. Compared with a recent share price of $33.71, this implies the stock is around 56.5% undervalued on this method.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Halliburton Price vs Earnings

For a business that is generating profits, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It gives you a quick sense of how the market currently values those earnings relative to other companies.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risks. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher multiple, while lower growth or higher uncertainty usually means investors are comfortable with a lower P/E.

Halliburton currently trades on a P/E of about 22.01x, compared with an Energy Services industry average of 25.27x and a peer group average of 21.86x. Simply Wall St also calculates a “Fair Ratio” of 22.64x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of the P/E that might be reasonable for Halliburton given its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market value and risk factors. It is designed to be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it builds in company specific characteristics rather than treating all firms as alike.

Compared with the current P/E of 22.01x, the Fair Ratio of 22.64x suggests Halliburton’s P/E is slightly below that model based estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED
